topics.title: There is / There are
topics.explanation: We use "there is" for one thing, and "there are" for more than one thing.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "there is" لشيء واحد، و"there are" لأكثر من شيء واحد.

examples.en: There is a book on the table.
There are three cats in the garden.
There isn't any milk in the fridge.
Are there any pencils in your bag?
examples.ar: هل يوجد أقلام رصاص في حقيبتك؟

topics.title: Comparatives (bigger, smaller)
topics.explanation: We use comparatives to compare two things. For short words, we usually add "-er": big → bigger, small → smaller.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم صيغة المقارنة لمقارنة شيئين. للكلمات القصيرة، نضيف عادة "-er": big → bigger.

examples.en: An elephant is bigger than a mouse.
My bag is heavier than yours.
This book is more interesting than that one.
examples.ar: هذا الكتاب أكثر تشويقًا من ذلك.

examples.en: Today is colder than yesterday.

topics.title: Plural Nouns
topics.explanation: Most nouns add "-s" to become plural (book → books), but some are irregular and change completely (child → children, man → men).
topics.explanationAr: معظم الأسماء تضيف "-s" لتصبح جمعًا، لكن بعضها شاذ ويتغير تمامًا.

examples.en: I have two brothers and three sisters.
There are many children in the park.
examples.ar: يوجد الكثير من الأطفال في الحديقة.

examples.en: I saw two mice in the kitchen.
These boxes are very heavy.

topics.title: Possessive Adjectives
topics.explanation: We use my, your, his, her, our, and their to show who something belongs to.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم my وyour وhis وher وour وtheir لبيان ملكية شيء ما.

examples.en: This is my pencil.
Her dress is blue.
Our teacher is very kind.
Their house is near the school.

topics.title: Prepositions of Place
topics.explanation: We use in, on, under, next to, and between to describe where something is.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم in وon وunder وnext to وbetween لوصف مكان الشيء.

examples.en: The cat is under the table.
My book is on the desk.
The shop is next to the bakery.
I am sitting between my two friends.

topics.title: Can / Can't (Ability)
topics.explanation: We use "can" to say someone is able to do something, and "can't" when they are not able to.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "can" للتعبير عن القدرة على فعل شيء، و"can't" لعدم القدرة.

examples.en: I can swim very well.
She can speak two languages.
He can't ride a bike yet.
examples.ar: هو لا يستطيع ركوب الدراجة بعد.

examples.en: Can you help me with my bag?

topics.title: This / That / These / Those
topics.explanation: "This" and "these" point to things that are near. "That" and "those" point to things that are far. Use "this/that" for one thing, "these/those" for more than one.
topics.explanationAr: "this" و"these" للأشياء القريبة. "that" و"those" للأشياء البعيدة.

examples.en: This is my new bag.
That is my friend's house over there.
These shoes are very comfortable.
Those birds are flying south.

topics.title: Wh- Questions
topics.explanation: We use question words like what, where, who, and when to ask for specific information.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم أدوات الاستفهام مثل what وwhere وwho وwhen للسؤال عن معلومات محددة.

examples.en: What is your name?
Where do you live?
Who is your best friend?
When is your birthday?
